excerpt: The lunar cycle encompasses twenty-eight mansions which correspond to the lunar month containing twenty-nine and one-half days precisely
The fractional half-days multiplied across twelve months accumulate to eighteen additional days, corresponding to the holiest observances within the sacred calendar

Hurr embodied duality: he was simultaneously among those who obstructed the Imam and among those who sacrificed themselves for him, exemplifying the principle of transformation from deficiency to perfection
When Hurr fell wounded upon the battlefield, the Imam bound his brow with a kerchief, declaring Your mother erred not in naming you Hurr—you are free in this world and free in the Hereafter
Minutes and hours, when gathered, transform deficiency into completeness, just as Hurr ascended from among the obstructing army to the most exalted martyr
source_location_hint: principle of kabīsa and spiritual elevation

excerpt: John, an enslaved Abyssinian, through devotion to the Imam, was transformed from foul appearance to radiance and perfume, exemplifying the principle of spiritual elevation
Salman al-Farisi, born a Persian foreigner, became of the intimate household of the Prophet, embodying the duality of transformation into the perfect day
When Sayyidna Jafar transmitted the text to Muhammad ibn Fahd, both achieved the station of dāʿī simultaneously during their joint lifetime, exemplifying dual perfection
Nine intellects were established in the celestial domain ordered solely by monotheism, but the Tenth Intellect—the First Intellect, the Emanator—bears the stations of repentance alongside monotheism
source_location_hint: hierarchy of intellects and cosmic emanation
